scope: all six HTML routes, shared navigation and identity system, responsive behavior, reduced motion, interactions, internal links, and all four PDFs
rootCause: Static KPMG identity was already present, while app.js loaded brand-runtime.js, which injected a second employer lockup and additional document/report brand bars.
defectsFoundAndRepaired: Removed the redundant runtime branding injector and deleted brand-runtime.js.
Rebuilt navigation as a candidate-only text identity so each route has exactly one KPMG employer lockup.
Unified all six route menus, active states, Escape behavior, outside-click close, and mobile layout.
Corrected the mobile active-route style that rendered white text on a transparent menu row.
Rebuilt resume and cover-letter headers as single identity systems with verified contact information.
Corrected the resume page-two continuation header and balanced the second page with verified technical evidence.
Corrected cover-letter target-role clipping and retained working candidate-vision, resume, and decision-lab hyperlinks.
Regenerated report PDFs from current branded HTML so the KPMG identifier is present in the downloads.
Added a compact KPMG continuation header to page two of the 90-day plan.
remainingGate: Verify the deployed GitHub Pages output after main propagation, including live reciprocal navigation and live PDF download responses.
